front_text: An ectopic pregnancy occurs when a fertilized egg implants outside the uterine cavity, preventing normal embryo development.
front_html: An ectopic pregnancy occurs when a fertilized egg implants {{c1::outside the uterine cavity}}, preventing normal embryo development.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Approximately 95% of ectopic pregnancies occur in the fallopian tube, most commonly in the ampulla (the widest section of the tube).
front_html: Approximately {{c1::95}}% of ectopic pregnancies occur in the {{c1::fallopian tube}}, most commonly in the {{c1::ampulla}} (the widest section of the tube).
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: The most common site of ectopic pregnancy within the fallopian tube is the ampulla, which is the widest section of the tube.
front_html: The most common site of ectopic pregnancy within the fallopian tube is the {{c1::ampulla}}, which is the widest section of the tube.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Ectopic pregnancy causes tubal rupture because the fallopian tube cannot expand like the uterus, leading to internal bleeding and a life-threatening emergency.
front_html: Ectopic pregnancy causes tubal rupture because the {{c1::fallopian tube}} cannot expand like the uterus, leading to internal bleeding and a life-threatening emergency.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: The classic triad of ectopic pregnancy consists of abdominal pain, vaginal bleeding, and amenorrhea.
front_html: The classic triad of ectopic pregnancy consists of {{c1::abdominal pain}}, {{c1::vaginal bleeding}}, and {{c1::amenorrhea}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: In ectopic pregnancy, abdominal pain is typically unilateral and may radiate to the shoulder or back if blood irritates the diaphragm after tubal rupture.
front_html: In ectopic pregnancy, abdominal pain is typically {{c1::unilateral}} and may radiate to the {{c1::shoulder}} or {{c1::back}} if blood irritates the diaphragm after tubal rupture.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Shoulder pain in ectopic pregnancy results from phrenic nerve irritation due to intra-abdominal bleeding.
front_html: Shoulder pain in ectopic pregnancy results from {{c1::phrenic nerve}} irritation due to {{c1::intra-abdominal bleeding}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: In ectopic pregnancy with significant bleeding, the patient may report dizziness, syncope, or weakness.
front_html: In ectopic pregnancy with significant bleeding, the patient may report {{c1::dizziness}}, {{c1::syncope}}, or {{c1::weakness}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: On physical exam, ectopic pregnancy may reveal tenderness, an adnexal mass, or signs of peritonitis such as rigid abdomen, guarding, and rebound tenderness.
front_html: On physical exam, ectopic pregnancy may reveal {{c1::tenderness}}, an {{c1::adnexal mass}}, or signs of {{c1::peritonitis}} such as rigid abdomen, guarding, and rebound tenderness.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: A ruptured ectopic pregnancy may present with signs of shock including tachycardia, hypotension, pallor, and orthostatic changes, constituting a medical emergency.
front_html: A ruptured ectopic pregnancy may present with signs of shock including {{c1::tachycardia}}, {{c1::hypotension}}, {{c1::pallor}}, and {{c1::orthostatic changes}}, constituting a medical emergency.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: The most common cause of ectopic pregnancy is tubal occlusion from adhesions, often secondary to pelvic inflammatory disease (PID).
front_html: The most common cause of ectopic pregnancy is {{c1::tubal occlusion}} from adhesions, often secondary to {{c1::pelvic inflammatory disease (PID)}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra:
Other risk factors:
• previous ectopic pregnancy
• IUD use (rare, but if pregnancy occurs with an IUD, it is more likely to be ectopic)
• smoking (affects tubal motility)
• endometriosis (can distort tubal anatomy)
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: A history of previous ectopic pregnancy is a risk factor for recurrent ectopic pregnancy.
front_html: A history of previous {{c1::ectopic pregnancy}} is a risk factor for recurrent ectopic pregnancy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra: Other risk factors: tubal occlusion from adhesions (often secondary to PID), IUD use (if pregnancy occurs with an IUD, more likely to be ectopic), smoking (affects tubal motility), endometriosis (can distort tubal anatomy)
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Intrauterine device (IUD) use is a risk factor for ectopic pregnancy; although pregnancy with an IUD is rare, when it occurs it is more likely to be ectopic.
front_html: {{c1::Intrauterine device (IUD)}} use is a risk factor for ectopic pregnancy; although pregnancy with an IUD is rare, when it occurs it is more likely to be ectopic.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra: Other risk factors: tubal occlusion from adhesions (often secondary to PID), previous ectopic pregnancy, smoking (affects tubal motility), endometriosis (can distort tubal anatomy)
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Smoking is a risk factor for ectopic pregnancy because it impairs tubal motility.
front_html: {{c1::Smoking}} is a risk factor for ectopic pregnancy because it impairs {{c1::tubal motility}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra: Other risk factors: tubal occlusion from PID adhesions, previous ectopic pregnancy, IUD use, endometriosis
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Endometriosis is a risk factor for ectopic pregnancy because it can distort tubal anatomy.
front_html: {{c1::Endometriosis}} is a risk factor for ectopic pregnancy because it can distort tubal anatomy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Diagnosis of ectopic pregnancy relies on a combination of history, β-hCG measurement, and transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS).
front_html: Diagnosis of ectopic pregnancy relies on a combination of history, {{c1::β-hCG}} measurement, and {{c1::transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS)}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) is >90% sensitive for detecting ectopic pregnancy.
front_html: Transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) is {{c1::>90}}% sensitive for detecting ectopic pregnancy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: On transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) in ectopic pregnancy, an empty uterus in the setting of an elevated β-hCG is a suspicious finding.
front_html: On transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) in ectopic pregnancy, an {{c1::empty uterus}} in the setting of an elevated β-hCG is a suspicious finding.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra:
Other TVUS findings:
• complex adnexal mass separate from the ovary
• "ring of fire" sign on Doppler imaging
• free fluid in pelvis or Morison's pouch suggesting rupture
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: On transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) in ectopic pregnancy, a complex adnexal mass separate from the ovary is a suspicious finding.
front_html: On transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) in ectopic pregnancy, a {{c1::complex adnexal mass}} separate from the ovary is a suspicious finding.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: The classic "ring of fire" sign on Doppler imaging in ectopic pregnancy represents a hypervascular ring surrounding the ectopic gestational sac due to increased trophoblastic blood flow.
front_html: The classic {{c1::"ring of fire"}} sign on Doppler imaging in ectopic pregnancy represents a {{c1::hypervascular ring}} surrounding the ectopic gestational sac due to increased {{c1::trophoblastic blood flow}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Free fluid in the pelvis or Morison's pouch (between the liver and kidney) on a transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) suggests rupture and intra-abdominal bleeding in ectopic pregnancy.
front_html: Free fluid in the pelvis or {{c1::Morison's pouch}} (between the liver and kidney) on a transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) suggests {{c1::rupture}} and intra-abdominal bleeding in ectopic pregnancy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: In a normal early pregnancy, β-hCG doubles every 48 hours, defined as an increase of ≥66 %, indicating a viable intrauterine pregnancy (IUP).
front_html: In a normal early pregnancy, β-hCG doubles every {{c1::48}} hours, defined as an increase of {{c1::≥66}} %, indicating a viable intrauterine pregnancy (IUP).
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: In an ectopic or nonviable pregnancy, β-hCG rises slower than expected or plateaus, for example increasing only 15–30 % in 48 hours instead of doubling.
front_html: In an ectopic or nonviable pregnancy, β-hCG rises {{c1::slower than expected}} or {{c1::plateaus}}, for example increasing only {{c1::15–30}} % in 48 hours instead of doubling.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: The discriminatory zone is the β-hCG level above which an intrauterine pregnancy should be visible on ultrasound. At β-hCG ~1,500–2,000 mIU/mL, a gestational sac should be visible on transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS).
front_html: The discriminatory zone is the β-hCG level above which an intrauterine pregnancy should be visible on ultrasound. At β-hCG {{c1::~1,500–2,000}} mIU/mL, a gestational sac should be visible on {{c1::transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS)}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: At β-hCG ~6,000–6,500 mIU/mL, a gestational sac should be visible on abdominal ultrasound.
front_html: At β-hCG {{c1::~6,000–6,500}} mIU/mL, a gestational sac should be visible on {{c1::abdominal ultrasound}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: When β-hCG is  mIU/mL and no gestational sac is seen, ectopic pregnancy cannot be excluded; the appropriate next step is to repeat β-hCG in 48 hours.
front_html: When β-hCG is {{c1::<1,500}} mIU/mL and no gestational sac is seen, ectopic pregnancy cannot be excluded; the appropriate next step is to repeat β-hCG in {{c1::48}} hours.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Methotrexate is a folic acid antagonist that inhibits DNA synthesis, stopping trophoblastic tissue growth and allowing the body to resorb the ectopic pregnancy without surgery.
front_html: Methotrexate is a {{c1::folic acid antagonist}} that inhibits {{c1::DNA synthesis}}, stopping trophoblastic tissue growth and allowing the body to resorb the ectopic pregnancy without surgery.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Methotrexate for ectopic pregnancy is typically administered intramuscularly (IM) as a single dose, though multi-dose regimens may be used.
front_html: Methotrexate for ectopic pregnancy is typically administered {{c1::intramuscularly (IM)}} as a {{c1::single dose}}, though multi-dose regimens may be used.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: After methotrexate treatment for ectopic pregnancy, β-hCG levels are monitored on days 4 and 7; a 15% decline between these days indicates treatment success.
front_html: After methotrexate treatment for ectopic pregnancy, β-hCG levels are monitored on days {{c1::4}} and {{c1::7}}; a {{c1::15}}% decline between these days indicates treatment success.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Following methotrexate treatment for ectopic pregnancy, the patient should avoid alcohol, folic acid supplements, and intercourse until β-hCG levels return to zero.
front_html: Following methotrexate treatment for ectopic pregnancy, the patient should avoid {{c1::alcohol}}, {{c1::folic acid supplements}}, and {{c1::intercourse}} until β-hCG levels return to zero.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: The preferred surgical approach for ectopic pregnancy is laparoscopic salpingostomy, in which ectopic tissue is removed and the tube is preserved when possible.
front_html: The preferred surgical approach for ectopic pregnancy is {{c1::laparoscopic salpingostomy}}, in which ectopic tissue is removed and the tube is preserved when possible.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra:
Other surgical options:
• salpingectomy if the tube is severely damaged or bleeding heavily
• laparotomy reserved for unstable or complex cases
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: A salpingectomy (removal of the tube) is required when the fallopian tube is severely damaged or bleeding heavily in ectopic pregnancy.
front_html: A {{c1::salpingectomy}} (removal of the tube) is required when the fallopian tube is severely damaged or bleeding heavily in ectopic pregnancy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra:
Other surgical options:
• laparoscopic salpingostomy (preferred, tube-preserving)
• laparotomy reserved for unstable or complex cases
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Laparotomy (open surgery) is reserved for unstable or complex ectopic pregnancy cases, while laparoscopy is associated with faster recovery.
front_html: {{c1::Laparotomy}} (open surgery) is reserved for {{c1::unstable}} or complex ectopic pregnancy cases, while laparoscopy is associated with faster recovery.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
extra:
Other surgical options:
• laparoscopic salpingostomy (preferred)
• salpingectomy if tube is severely damaged or bleeding heavily
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: A ruptured ectopic pregnancy is a true surgical emergency presenting with sudden severe abdominal pain, referred shoulder pain, syncope, and signs of shock including low blood pressure and high heart rate.
front_html: A ruptured ectopic pregnancy is a true surgical emergency presenting with sudden severe abdominal pain, referred {{c1::shoulder pain}}, {{c1::syncope}}, and signs of shock including {{c1::low blood pressure}} and {{c1::high heart rate}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Management of a ruptured ectopic pregnancy requires immediate resuscitation with IV fluids and blood, followed by emergency laparotomy for bleeding control and removal of the ectopic pregnancy.
front_html: Management of a ruptured ectopic pregnancy requires immediate resuscitation with {{c1::IV fluids}} and {{c1::blood}}, followed by emergency {{c1::laparotomy}} for bleeding control and removal of the ectopic pregnancy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy

front_text: Delay in recognizing a ruptured ectopic pregnancy can lead to death from hemorrhagic shock.
front_html: Delay in recognizing a ruptured ectopic pregnancy can lead to death from {{c1::hemorrhagic shock}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Ectopic pregnancy
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Ectopic pregnancy
